content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 26, 1989

Teresa Craigie
Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro
Post Office Box 7880
San Francisco, CA  94120

Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
     Our File No. I-89-674

Dear Ms. Craigie:

     We have received your letter of November 20, 1989 (copy
attached) seeking written confirmation of the advice I provided in
our recent telephone conversation. This is to confirm that your
letter accurately reflects that advice.

     If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General, Counsel

                                        [illegible]
                                        By:  John G. McLean
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

KED/JGM/aa

Attachment

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

LAW OFFICES OF

PILLSBURY, MADISON & SUTRO
POST OFFICE BOX 7880

OTHER OFFICES

LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA
SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA
WALNUT CREEK, CALIFORNIA
WASHINGTON, D.C.

SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94120

TELEPHONE (415) 983-1000

CABLE ADDRESS "EVANS"

TELEX 34743
TELECOPIER (415) 398-2096

WRITER'S OFFICE AND
DIRECT DIAL NUMBER

235 Montgomery Street
(415) 983-1968

November 20, 1989


VIA FEDERAL EXPRESS

Mr. John McLean
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices
Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear John:

     The purpose of this letter is to confirm your
informal oral advice.

     I explained that, from time to time, our clients
may provide services or advice to a local government agency
which could be characterized as a "gift" to one or more of
the members of the local governmental agency. For example,
in one instance, a corporation was going to pay for the
services of an advisor who would prepare a report for a local
governmental agency on governmental administration and would
provide some preliminary guidance on administrative issues.

     It was your opinion that, in this situation, the
corporate payment would probably not be a "gift" to any
public official under Section 82028 of the Government Code.
You explained that, although the governmental agency would
benefit from the advisor's services, no agency member would
receive any personal benefit therefrom. However, you said
that, without obtaining formal written advice from the
Commission to that effect, it would be prudent to comply

Mr. John McLean
November 20, 1989
Page 2

with the provisions of the Stone Opinion (3 FPPC Opinions
52).

Very truly yours,

Teresa Craigie
Teresa Craigie


I agree with the foregoing.


John McLean
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission




10263896

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 29, 1989


Teresa Craigie
Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro
P.O. Box 7880
San Francisco, CA  94120

Re:  Letter No. 89-674

Dear Ms. Craigie:

     We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on November 27, 1989. Your letter
has been assigned to John McLean, an attorney in the Legal
Division, for response. If you have any questions, you may
contact him directly at (916) 322-5901.

     If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. A confirming
response to the letter will be released after it has gone through our approval
process. If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you.  In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

     You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh:confadv1


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question: Whether a corporation's payment for services or advice provided to a local governmental agency (such as an advisor preparing a report on governmental administration) constitutes a "gift" to public officials under Section 82028 of the Government Code.
sections.conclusion: It was your opinion that, in this situation, the corporate payment would probably not be a "gift" to any public official under Section 82028 of the Government Code. You explained that, although the governmental agency would benefit from the advisor's services, no agency member would receive any personal benefit therefrom. However, you said that, without obtaining formal written advice from the Commission to that effect, it would be prudent to comply with the provisions of the Stone Opinion (3 FPPC Opinions 52).

sections.question_synthetic: Whether corporate payment for advisory services provided to a local governmental agency constitutes a gift to public officials under the Political Reform Act. The inquiry specifically addresses whether an agency receiving benefits from an advisor's services means individual agency members receive personal benefits triggering gift restrictions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that corporate payments for advisory services to a local governmental agency likely do not constitute gifts to public officials because individual agency members receive no personal benefit, even though the agency itself benefits. However, the FPPC advised prudent compliance with the Stone Opinion standards pending formal written advice.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document includes multiple related letters: the initial FPPC confirmation letter (12/26/89), the incoming request letter from Craigie confirming oral advice (11/20/89), and an acknowledgment letter (11/29/89). The question and conclusion are derived from Craigie's letter describing the prior oral advice that was confirmed. This is a confirmation of informal oral advice rather than a full formal advice letter. OCR quality is generally good but some signatures are unclear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a corporation's payment for services or advice provided to a local governmental agency (such as an advisor preparing a report on governmental administration) constitutes a "gift" to public officials under Section 82028 of the Government Code.

CONCLUSION: It was your opinion that, in this situation, the corporate payment would probably not be a "gift" to any public official under Section 82028 of the Government Code. You explained that, although the governmental agency would benefit from the advisor's services, no agency member would receive any personal benefit therefrom. However, you said that, without obtaining formal written advice from the Commission to that effect, it would be prudent to comply with the provisions of the Stone Opinion (3 FPPC Opinions 52).

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC confirmation letter (No. 89-674) from December 1989 responding to a request by attorney Teresa Craigie to confirm informal oral advice regarding whether corporate payments for advisory services to local governmental agencies constitute gifts under the Political Reform Act.
